title: Add BitNet b1.58 Ternary Quantization (non-record submission)
body:
val_bpb: 1.2185 (3-seed mean, std 0.0018)

First submission using BitNet-style ternary quantization {-1, 0, +1} instead of int6. Ternary uses ~1.58 bits/weight vs 6 bits/weight, allowing 2x more parameters (67.5M vs ~33M) in the same size budget.

Key findings:
- EMA is incompatible with ternary (causes +0.52 bpb degradation)
- Ternary QAT overhead results in 214ms/step vs SOTA's 84ms/step
- Despite 2x more parameters, fewer training steps limits performance
